title: Standardised Immunology Laboratory Protocols:  A Comprehensive Guide for Experimental Techniques
author: Mathur, Vedant
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This paper provides a step-by-step guide for essential immunology laboratory techniques,  helping students understand and perform key experiments with accuracy. The experiments  covered include ABO-Rh blood grouping, plasma and serum separation, peripheral blood  mononuclear cell (PBMC) isolation, and immunological assays such as Single Radial  Immunodiffusion (SRID), Rocket Immunoelectrophoresis, Counter-Current Electrophoresis,  and ELISA techniques (Dot ELISA and Sandwich ELISA). Additionally, protein analysis  techniques like SDS-PAGE and Western Blotting are included to help students study antigen antibody interactions and protein separation. Each experiment follows a structured approach,  explaining the principle, materials required, procedure, observations, and interpretation of  results. The ABO-Rh blood grouping experiment teaches students how to identify blood types  using slide-based agglutination. Plasma and serum separation demonstrates the use of  centrifugation to differentiate blood components. PBMC isolation using density gradient  centrifugation is essential for immunological research and cell culture. Single Radial  Immunodiffusion (SRID) and Rocket Immunoelectrophoresis allow for quantification of  antigens, while Counter-Current Electrophoresis enables rapid detection of antibodies. Dot  ELISA and Sandwich ELISA are techniques used for detecting specific proteins in biological  samples. SDS-PAGE and Western Blotting provide insights into protein separation and  identification
